expectations.text: Treats this correctly as the agency's incident too: activate the agency IR plan, establish what agency data/classification the supplier holds, and demand supplier containment/forensics status under the contract
expectations.evidence: "Activate your agency's incident response plan immediately, treating this as a confirmed (not suspected) security incident"; "what agency data was held... the classification level of the affected data... containment/eradication status"

expectations.text: Names the right NZ notification channel for cyber incidents: report to the NCSC (GCSB) — noting CERT NZ's functions are now within the NCSC — plus NZ Police for the criminal act where appropriate
expectations.evidence: Response lists NCSC and CERT NZ as two separate, parallel bodies that merely "coordinate" ("CERT NZ — for broader incident reporting/coordination... CERT NZ and NCSC coordinate on national incident response") rather than stating CERT NZ's functions are now absorbed within NCSC. NZ Police is never mentioned anywhere in the response.

expectations.text: Identifies the Privacy Act 2020 obligation: if personal information is involved and the breach is notifiable (serious harm threshold), notify the Office of the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals
expectations.evidence: "Office of the Privacy Commissioner (OPC)... if the compromised data includes personal information... likely to cause serious harm, this is a notifiable privacy breach... requires notification to the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals"; separately lists "Affected individuals — if personal information compromised... individuals must be notified under the Privacy Act 2020"

expectations.text: Covers evidence preservation and reporting discipline: preserve logs/artifacts, document a timeline, and conduct a post-incident review feeding lessons back into supplier controls
expectations.evidence: "Preserve evidence and maintain a timeline/incident log"; "Document lessons learned and update supplier risk assessments/due diligence processes"; "Complete a formal incident report for internal governance, capturing timeline, impact, notifications made, and remediation actions"

expectations.text: Addresses the supplier-contract dimension: invoke incident-notification clauses, require remediation evidence, reassess the supplier's certification/assurance status, and update the agency risk register
expectations.evidence: Covers notification clauses ("Review your contract with the supplier for mandatory breach notification clauses"), remediation evidence ("Require the supplier to provide a formal incident report... remediation actions"), and risk register update ("supplier risk register"), but never mentions reassessing the supplier's certification or assurance status — no occurrence of 'certif' or 'assurance' in the response.
